title: First-play onboarding and next-action guidance
summary: Make first play legible with phase, possible actions, next goal and game-versus-diagnostics separation.
acceptance: Default UI explains current phase, available actions and next goal in zh-CN/en
Separates gameplay guidance from developer diagnostics
Extends M7 onboarding into M8 first-play guidance without claiming final release
Records copy limits and cultural terminology boundaries
Covered by tests or reviewer-verifiable UI evidence

history.details: Created by WM-0111 M8 UI/i18n scope amendment DAG; remains proposed until dependencies are done.

Implemented M8 first-play guidance on the default player start surface with zh-CN/en copy, next-goal and available-action UI evidence, diagnostics separation, and copy/cultural boundary documentation; required checks pass.

changes_requested: High: default first-play guidance still renders Product Gate terminology in the player start surface boundary copy, so developer diagnostics leak into onboarding; Medium: zh-CN first-play evidence only verifies localized headings while phase/next-goal values come from raw English read-model labels/details, leaving zh-CN next-action guidance unverified.

Repaired WM-0119 review findings: removed developer-gate terminology from default first-play copy, added localized zh-CN phase and next-goal display derivation, added UI/Web/Desktop assertions against raw English fixture leakage, and reran required checks plus Web/Desktop e2e.

verified: No blocking findings. Second-round repairs verified: default first-play copy no longer exposes Product Gate/developer-gate terminology, Product Gate remains only in explicit diagnostics/dev keys and assertions, zh-CN start-surface tests assert localized phase/next-goal/action details and reject raw English fixture leakage, and no sim-core/sim-worker/protocol or release-gated paths changed; required checks plus Web/Desktop e2e pass.
Integrated verified M8 first-play guidance after second-round repair; checks passed: handoff validate, taskctl validate/status, diff check, Web/Desktop E2E, quality.

review.summary: No blocking findings. Second-round repairs verified: default first-play copy no longer exposes Product Gate/developer-gate terminology, Product Gate remains only in explicit diagnostics/dev keys and assertions, zh-CN start-surface tests assert localized phase/next-goal/action details and reject raw English fixture leakage, and no sim-core/sim-worker/protocol or release-gated paths changed; required checks plus Web/Desktop e2e pass.

integration.summary: Integrated verified M8 first-play guidance after second-round repair; checks passed: handoff validate, taskctl validate/status, diff check, Web/Desktop E2E, quality.
